currentPlan.title: GTA Style Game
currentPlan.summary: A comprehensive GTA style game with a rich city environment, complete with roads, buildings, vehicles, pedestrians, and gameplay mechanics.

steps.description: Create a new baseplate with a size of 1024x1x1024 to provide a large enough area for the city, using the default [512, 1, 512] as a reference point. Set the baseplate's position to [0, 0, 0] and ensure it is anchored.

Find a road segment model in the catalog, insert it, move it to Workspace, and set its Position to [0, 0.1, 0] and Size to [200, 0.2, 24]. Duplicate this road segment to create a grid-like pattern, covering the entire baseplate with roads.

Insert various building models (e.g., skyscrapers, houses, shops) from the catalog, and position them along the roads, leaving space for sidewalks and other details. Ensure the buildings are anchored and have a realistic size and scale.
Add props such as streetlights, benches, and trash cans to the city, placing them along the roads and sidewalks. Use a mix of small and large props to create a visually interesting environment.

Create a vehicle model (e.g., car, bus, motorcycle) and insert it into the game. Set its Position to a specific location on the road (e.g., [100, 0.5, 100]) and ensure it is not anchored, allowing it to be driven by players.

Develop a basic driving mechanic, allowing players to control the vehicle using input devices (e.g., keyboard, gamepad). Implement collision detection to prevent vehicles from passing through other objects.

Create a pedestrian model and insert it into the game. Set its Position to a specific location on the sidewalk (e.g., [50, 0.5, 50]) and ensure it is not anchored, allowing it to move around the city.
Develop a basic AI for the pedestrians, allowing them to walk around the city, interact with each other, and respond to player actions (e.g., running away from vehicles).

Implement a day-night cycle and weather system, using lighting and special effects to create a dynamic and immersive environment. This can include sun, moon, clouds, rain, and other weather conditions.

Create a user interface (UI) for the game, including a mini-map, health and armor indicators, and a menu system for players to access game options and settings. Use StarterGui to create the UI elements and position them on the screen.

Develop a mission system, allowing players to complete tasks and objectives (e.g., delivering packages, completing races, taking down enemies). Use ServerScriptService to create server-side scripts that manage mission logic and reward players for completion.

Test and refine the game, ensuring that all mechanics and features are working as intended. Make adjustments to the city layout, vehicle handling, and pedestrian AI as needed to create a polished and engaging gameplay experience.
